verdict.answer_first: A leads with the fault: "unguarded property access" and the exact failure; B starts broader: "Yes, a few things".
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one primary fix: "return u?.profile?.name ?? null"; B adds return-null, throw NotFoundError, rename, validate id, caller contract.
verdict.fabrication: B overstates: "propagates as an unhandled rejection unless the caller wraps it"; a rejected async function is not inherently unhandled if the returned promise is handled. A: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply: one bug, one safer version, one contract caveat. B is useful but diluted by extra hardening.
